context_update.summary: The front matter chunk provides the article title, abstract, and the beginning of introduction. It introduces the research on three hot-rolled austenitic low-density steels: Fe-28Mn-9Al-0.9C (Ni-free), Fe-28Mn-9Al-5Ni-0.9C (Ni high-Mn), and Fe-15Mn-9Al-5Ni-0.9C (Ni low-Mn). The abstract summarizes key findings: Ni-free alloy has γ-matrix and fine κ-carbides; Ni low-Mn alloy has coarser κ-carbides and B2 phases with bamboo-like stringer and polygonal morphologies; B2-containing alloys show higher strength but lower ductility; nanoindentation reveals similar hardness of γ and B2 with distinct pop-in events in B2; co-deformation near yield and orientation-relationship-dependent slip transfer control ductility; strain hardening differences are attributed to dislocation substructure evolution. The introduction defines four microstructural features of austenitic LDS (γ, κ, B2, DO3) and classifies B2 morphology into three types. This chunk establishes the material systems, sample aliases, processing route (hot-rolled), key phases, and initial property trends to be retained for subsequent sections.
microstructure: coarser κ-carbides + B2 (bamboo-like stringer and polygonal) in Ni low-Mn
B2 morphology classification: stringers, intergranular spherical (200 nm - 1 μm), intragranular (50-300 nm)
properties: B2-containing alloys: higher strength, reduced tensile ductility
mechanisms: γ and B2 co-deform near yield
non-favourable OR causes strain accumulation and B2 cracking
dislocation substructure evolution differences explain strain hardening

rolling_context_summary:
Summary: The front matter chunk provides the article title, abstract, and the beginning of introduction. It introduces the research on three hot-rolled austenitic low-density steels: Fe-28Mn-9Al-0.9C (Ni-free), Fe-28Mn-9Al-5Ni-0.9C (Ni high-Mn), and Fe-15Mn-9Al-5Ni-0.9C (Ni low-Mn). The abstract summarizes key findings: Ni-free alloy has γ-matrix and fine κ-carbides; Ni low-Mn alloy has coarser κ-carbides and B2 phases with bamboo-like stringer and polygonal morphologies; B2-containing alloys show higher strength but lower ductility; nanoindentation reveals similar hardness of γ and B2 with distinct pop-in events in B2; co-deformation near yield and orientation-relationship-dependent slip transfer control ductility; strain hardening differences are attributed to dislocation substructure evolution. The introduction defines four microstructural features of austenitic LDS (γ, κ, B2, DO3) and classifies B2 morphology into three types. This chunk establishes the material systems, sample aliases, processing route (hot-rolled), key phases, and initial property trends to be retained for subsequent sections.
Material systems: Fe-28Mn-9Al-0.9C; Fe-28Mn-9Al-5Ni-0.9C; Fe-15Mn-9Al-5Ni-0.9C
Sample aliases: Ni-free LDS: Fe-28Mn-9Al-0.9C; Ni high-Mn LDS: Fe-28Mn-9Al-5Ni-0.9C; Ni low-Mn LDS: Fe-15Mn-9Al-5Ni-0.9C
Processing route: hot-rolled
Key variables: Mn content; Ni content; B2 presence
Microstructure: γ-matrix + fine κ-carbides in Ni-free; coarser κ-carbides + B2 (bamboo-like stringer and polygonal) in Ni low-Mn; B2 morphology classification: stringers, intergranular spherical (200 nm - 1 μm), intragranular (50-300 nm)
Properties: B2-containing alloys: higher strength, reduced tensile ductility; γ and B2 similar hardness; pop-in events in B2 nanoindentation
Mechanisms: γ and B2 co-deform near yield; favourable OR enables slip transfer; non-favourable OR causes strain accumulation and B2 cracking; dislocation substructure evolution differences explain strain hardening
